summary: The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing on Zoning Case 2026-005, a text amendment to create a new M3 Limited High Impact Industrial District in the zoning resolution. The M3 district is designed as a precautionary measure to tightly regulate high-impact industrial uses such as data centers by restricting them to large, remote sites with extensive setbacks, buffers, and conditional use requirements. No specific M3 development is proposed; the amendment provides a legal framework to prevent exclusionary zoning challenges. The commission unanimously approved the text amendment for recommendation to the Board of Trustees.
decisions: Approved minutes from the May 5, 2026 meeting (moved by Dave, seconded by Lee, passed unanimously)

Approved Zoning Case 2026-005 M3 Limited High Impact Industrial District text amendments (Exhibits A through M) for recommendation to the Board of Trustees (moved by Lee Lewis, seconded by Ms. Purcell, passed unanimously 4-0)
action_items: Staff to forward the approved M3 text amendment recommendation (Exhibits A through M) to the Board of Trustees for final action
Staff to prepare two zoning cases for the July 7, 2026 meeting
key_amounts: 150 acres minimum site size for M3 district
500 feet minimum site width for M3 district
40% maximum lot coverage (60% open space required)
300 feet minimum setback from public right-of-way
750 feet minimum setback next to residential districts
1,000 feet minimum no-go zone from any residential district, town center, churches, schools, community centers, hospitals, cemeteries, and active parks
300 feet minimum side and rear setback next to business or industrial districts
50 hours per generator per year maximum for certain operations
notable_topics: Creation of M3 Limited High Impact Industrial District text amendment
Legal rationale for regulating rather than banning high-impact industrial uses
Minimum site size, setback, and buffer requirements for M3 district
Conditional use requirements for high-impact uses including data centers
Closure of PD overlay and PDM back doors for high-impact uses
Environmental and operational standards (noise, vibration, odor, dust, lighting, hazardous materials)
Data center-specific requirements for power and water
attendees: Mr. Dinwiddie (absent - no response at roll call)
Mr. Frisbee (absent - no response at roll call)
notes: This case was continued from the May 5, 2026 meeting to allow staff and legal counsel to verify internal consistency of the text across Exhibits A through M. Mr. Dinwiddie and Mr. Frisbee appear to have been absent as no response was recorded at roll call. The chairman emphasized on the record that no M3 development has been proposed and no developer has approached the township; this is a preemptive regulatory measure. The meeting was brief, adjourning at 6:49 PM. Two zoning cases are expected for the next meeting on July 7, 2026. Some portions of the transcript appear garbled or corrupted, particularly around data center utility requirements and operational standards.
title: ZC 6-2-26
